title: Heterotis decumbens
taxonomy: Heterotis decumbens is a species of flowering plant in the family Tiliaceae, commonly known as the sandalwood family. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. As a member of the Tiliaceae family, Heterotis decumbens is closely related to other plants such as the triad formation and the tilioid family. The Tiliaceae family is part of the larger order Malvales, which includes over 2000 species of flowering plants. These plants are known for their distinctive flowers and leaves, and are often found in humid and subtropical environments.
